Real det ()
 

Detailed Description

template<typename T>
class LUSolver< T >

Solve the linear system using the factorization.

Author
Daniele A. Di Pietro
Date
2006-07-29

The dense solver can be used in one of the following ways:

  • if the matrix needn't be preserved after computation and the associated system has to be solved only once, simply call solve(A, b). At exit, the argument A will contain the factorization of the original matrix. In order to avoid vector/matrix copies, the overwrite_solve() members may be used instead, the sole difference being that the solution is overwritten over the rhs vector/matrix. When the matrix passed as an argument is stored column-wise, the and factors are overwritten in the lower and upper triangular part of respectively. When is stored row-wise, the and factors are stored in the lower and upper triangular part of respectively.
  • if the matrix needs to be preserved after the computation or the associated system has to be solved several times, consider using a two-step strategy by first invoking factor(A), which makes a local copy of the matrix and factor it, and the calling solve(b) member, which actually solves the associated system. At exit, the matrix A will be preserved and its factorization will be stored in the LUSolver object. When solve() is replaced by overwrite_solve(), the solution is overwritten on the provided argument.
